question.text: If an investment of Rs.1,000 per month earns 15% per annum for 35 years, the approximate corpus accumulated would be:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** FV(15%/12, 420, -1000, 0, 0) = approximately Rs.1,46,77,180. The total contribution is only Rs.4,20,000 over 35 years, but compounding at 15% creates a massive corpus. **The Trap:** Rs.42,00,000 is just the simple sum of contributions (Rs.1,000 × 420 months). Many underestimate the power of compounding over long periods. **Cross-Exam Context:** The specific example of Rs.1,000/month at 15% for 35 years is directly from the NISM XVII workbook. Similar compounding examples appear in Series V-A. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This is the most powerful illustration of starting early — Rs.4.2 lakh of own money becomes Rs.1.47 crore. A 5-year delay (starting at 30 instead of 25) more than halves the corpus.
